task: I need to send a short update to a group after a delay has already caused some frustration, and I want it to be clear enough that people know what’s happening next. Can you help me shape a message that stays on track if they keep asking the same question?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it short, clear, and repeat the same core point.

A good structure is:

1. acknowledge the delay
2. say what’s being checked
3. give the next update time
4. avoid extra detail unless asked

You could send something like:

> “I understand the delay has been frustrating. We’re still confirming the remaining details before moving forward, and I’ll share the next update by [time]. I’m not going to guess at the answer before it’s confirmed.”

If they keep asking the same question, stay on the same line:

> “I hear you. I’m still waiting on confirmation, and I’ll update you as soon as I have it by [time].”

If you want, I can also turn this into:
- a more neutral version
- a firmer version
- a Slack/email version

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically the right shape. Short, calm, no extra fluff. The “I’m not going to guess” line helps if people are already annoyed.

The only thing I’d tighten is making the next step a little more concrete if you can. So instead of sounding like you’re just waiting around, make it clear what’s being checked and when they’ll hear again.

Something like:

“I know this delay has been frustrating. We’re still confirming the remaining details needed to move this forward, and I’ll send the next update by [time]. I won’t guess before it’s confirmed.”

If they keep pushing, just repeat the same point. Don’t keep adding new explanations.
